prs.title: fix(ai-ml): normalize evaluator ids and consolidate semantic evaluator
prs.body:
## Summary

Normalize evaluator identifier resolution in the aggregation step so weights map correctly, and consolidate duplicate semantic evaluator implementations into a single robust evaluator with retries, circuit-breaker, caching, and rate-limit fallback.

## Type of Change

- [ ] Feature
- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Refactor
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] Chore

## Related Issue

Closes #1072 

## Changes Made

* Updated `ai-ml/pipeline/aggregator.js` to normalize `name`, `key`, and `label` into camelCase, and attach the resolved name and weight to evaluator results so the `weights.config.js` mapping is applied correctly.

* Replaced `ai-ml/evaluators/semanticEvaluator.js` with a consolidated implementation featuring:

  * retry and backoff handling,
  * an in-process circuit breaker,
  * cache read/write support,
  * and a consistent result contract (`rateLimit` fallback now returns `score: null` with `meta.rateLimited`).

* Removed the duplicate implementation file: `ai-ml/pipeline/SemanticEvaluator.js`.


## Validation

- [ ] Build passes locally
- [ ] Relevant tests added/updated

prs.title: feat: opt-in Socket.IO per-socket rate limiter with warnings & cooldown
prs.body:
## Summary

Add an opt-in Socket.IO per-socket rate limiter to protect from message floods, with a soft rate_warning and cooldown before rate_limited disconnect. Add env validation and an integration test.

## Type of Change

- [x] Feature
- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] Chore

## Related Issue

Closes #1051 

## Changes Made

* Added an optional token-bucket socket rate limiter in `server/src/middleware/socketRateLimiter.js`.

* Added pre-disconnect `rate_warning` events with cooldown support via:

  * `SOCKET_RATE_WARNING_COOLDOWN_MS`
  * `SOCKET_RATE_WARNING_PERCENT`

* Added `SOCKET_RATE_ENABLED` flag to enable/disable the limiter (enabled by default).

* Added environment variable validation for all limiter-related configs in `server/src/config/validateEnv.js`.

* Added an integration test (`server/test/socketRateLimiter.test.js`) that simulates socket flooding and verifies limiter behavior.

* Updated `.env.example` with all new limiter environment variables and default values.

* Added `socket.io-client` as a dev dependency for integration testing in `server/package.json`.

* Removed temporary helper logger file to keep the implementation surface minimal and clean.

## Validation

- [x] Build passes locally
- [x] Relevant tests added/updated

prs.title: Robust batch processing — Celery chord + safe fallback
prs.body:
## What does this PR do?

Replaces request-bound batch processing with a reliable asynchronous workflow for image analysis.

The new implementation uses Celery `group` and `chord` primitives to process images independently via `analyze_image_task`, with automatic retries, exponential backoff, and a `finalize_batch_job` callback for batch completion handling.

To ensure consistency and recoverability, batch processing is idempotent: `AnalysisResult` records are created or updated per image, and batch statistics are recalculated server-side. The API now enforces a configurable batch size limit through `AGRI_MAX_BATCH_SIZE` (default: 200) and rejects oversized uploads.

When Celery is unavailable, the system falls back to a safe non-blocking in-process execution model using a background thread and thread pool. Error handling and logging have also been improved, with task outcomes persisted to the database and `/api/batch_upload` returning immediately with a `job_id` while processing continues asynchronously.


## Related Issue
Closes #492 

## Changes made
- ### `celery_tasks.py`

* Added `analyze_image_task`, `process_batch_job`, and `finalize_batch_job`.
* Implemented Celery `group` + `chord` orchestration.
* Added retry and backoff mechanisms for task execution.
* Added in-process fallback worker for environments without Celery.

- ### `app.py`

* Updated `/api/batch_upload` to enforce `MAX_BATCH_SIZE`.
* Dispatches batch processing through Celery when available.
* Falls back to background execution when Celery is unavailable.
* Returns a non-blocking response containing the batch `job_id`.


## Checklist
- [ ] I have tested my changes
- [ ] My code follows project guidelines
- [ ] No new errors introduced

prs.title: Require SECRET_KEY in production and harden session cookies
prs.body:
## What does this PR do?
Prevent accidental insecure deployments by requiring a non-empty `SECRET_KEY` when running in production. The app now validates the secret early and aborts startup if it is missing. It also applies safer defaults for session cookies and updates documentation and compose config so operators must supply a secret.

## Related Issue
Closes #419 

## Changes made
- app.py: validate `SECRET_KEY` at import time and raise SystemExit when `FLASK_ENV=production`.
- app.py: set sensible defaults for `SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE`, `SESSION_COOKIE_HTTPONLY`, and `SESSION_COOKIE_SAMESITE`.
- docker-compose.yml: removed any baked-in/unsafe dev-secret so deployments must provide a secret.
- .env.example / README.md: added concise instructions to generate and store a production `SECRET_KEY`.
- tests/test_config.py: add small import-time test that asserts the app aborts when `SECRET_KEY` is missing in production.

## Checklist
- [x] I have tested my changes
- [x] My code follows project guidelines
- [x] No new errors introduced

prs.title: Fix 2048 high score file path handling
prs.body:
Closes #253 

## Summary
This PR makes the 2048 game’s high score handling more reliable and portable.

The score file is now resolved relative to the script location instead of the current working directory, which prevents the game from reading or writing the wrong file when launched from another path. It also handles empty or invalid score contents safely by falling back to 0 rather than crashing on startup.

Validation was completed on the updated file, and no syntax errors were found.

prs.title: fix: remove duplicate Escape key listener in ExportOverlay to prevent double execution and memory leak
prs.body:
## Overview
Removes duplicate `useEffect` hook that was registering the Escape key event handler twice in `ExportOverlay.tsx`, causing the cancel callback to execute twice and creating a memory leak on component remount.

## Changes
- Removed redundant `useEffect` block that duplicated the Escape key listener logic
- Retained the first `useEffect` which properly implements the handler with `useCallback` and cleanup

## Why
The duplicate effect was causing:
1. **Double execution** — Escape key triggered `onCancel?.()` twice, risking race conditions
2. **Memory leak** — Each component mount added listeners without deduplication; unmount removed them, but remounting accumulated more
3. **Code duplication** — Identical logic in two separate effects

## Files Changed
- `src/components/ExportOverlay.tsx`

Closes #457

prs.title: Harden search history and FLAMES against DOM XSS
prs.body:
**Summary**

This PR removes unsafe HTML rendering from two user-facing paths in the web app.

The recent-search dropdown in web-app/js/main.js now builds DOM nodes and uses text-safe rendering instead of injecting search terms into HTML. The FLAMES result view in web-app/js/projects/flames.js now renders entered names and result content with textContent and created elements rather than innerHTML.

**Why this matters**

Both areas accepted user-controlled input and wrote it into the page as HTML, which created a DOM-based XSS risk. A crafted search term or name could inject markup into the app and execute in the browser.

**What changed**

* Replaced HTML interpolation in recent-search rendering with safe DOM construction.
* Replaced HTML interpolation in FLAMES result rendering with text-safe nodes.
* Kept the existing UI layout and behavior intact.

Fixes #192

prs.title: Fix: Prevent division by zero in Typing Speed Tester WPM calculation
prs.body:
## Problem
The typing speed tester crashes when users submit immediately (time_taken ≈ 0), 
causing a ZeroDivisionError or infinity WPM value.

## Solution
Added a guard clause to safely handle zero/near-zero time values:
- If time_taken > 0: Calculate WPM normally
- If time_taken ≤ 0: Set WPM to 0 (graceful fallback)

## Changes
- Modified `utilities/Typing-Speed-Tester/Typing-Speed-Tester.py` 
- Changed: `wpm = (words / time_taken) * 60` To: `wpm = (words / time_taken) * 60 if time_taken > 0 else 0`

Fixes #172

prs.title: Fix: Fibonacci Series returns incorrect number of terms
prs.body:
## Problem
The Fibonacci Series generator had an off-by-one error. When user requested 5 terms, it returned 8 terms instead.

## Root Cause
- Initialized series with `[0, 1, 1]` (3 elements)
- Loop condition `while i <= n` added additional elements
- Result: For n=5, total output = 3 + 5 = 8 terms

## Solution
- Simplified initialization to `[0, 1]`
- Changed loop to `while len(series) < n` to generate exactly n terms
- Uses cleaner logic with list slicing instead of multiple variable tracking

## Changes
- Removed unnecessary variable tracking (`num1`, `num2`, `num3`, `i`)
- Simplified logic to 1 line in loop
- More maintainable and readable code

Fixes #136

prs.title: Fix possible division by zero in radial geometric multiscale COLLECT mapping

labels.name: bug
labels.description: preCICE does not behave the way we want and we should look into it (and fix it if possible)

labels.name: GSoC
labels.description: Contributed in the context of the Google Summer of Code

prs.body:
## Main changes of this PR
This patch adds a validation step in `RadialGeoMultiscaleMapping::computeMapping()` to ensure that every output bin in COLLECT mode has at least one contributing input vertex.

## Motivation and additional information
During COLLECT mapping, output values are averaged using `_vertexCounter[i]` as divisor.  
If an output vertex receives no projected input vertices (e.g. due to sparse projection or mesh configuration), this results in a division by zero during mapping.
<!--
Short rational why preCICE needs this change. If this is already described in an issue a link to that issue (closes #123) is sufficient.
-->

prs.title: Fix macOS compatibility in tools/log.sh

labels.name: GSoC
labels.description: Contributed in the context of the Google Summer of Code

prs.body:
<!-- Please submit your Pull Request to the `develop` branch.
It may help to have a look at the file `CONTRIBUTING.md` for a few hints and guidelines. -->

## Summary 

tools/log.sh used GNU-specific flags that are not available on macOS.

This change makes the script portable by:

- Replacing `date --rfc-email` with a helper that falls back to a compatible `date` format when the flag is unavailable.
- Replacing `tee --append` with the portable `tee -a`.

The behavior of the script remains the same, but it now works correctly on macOS systems.

---

- [ ] I added a summary of any user-facing changes (compared to the last release) in the `changelog-entries/<PRnumber>.md`.
- [x] I will remember to squash-and-merge, providing a useful summary of the changes of this PR.

prs.title: Fix: Add null/undefined checks for status matrix parent block access
prs.body:
## Problem

Some blocks were accessing blockList[connections[0]].name in status matrix mode without checking whether the parent connection actually exists.

If a block has no parent (or the parent ID is invalid), this can throw a TypeError.

## Fix

Added null/undefined checks before accessing the parent block in these status matrix conditions.

## Changes

Updated 20 instances across:

- GraphicsBlocks.js

- BoxesBlocks.js

- MeterBlocks.js

- PenBlocks.js

- RhythmBlocks.js

- IntervalsBlocks.js

## Notes

This is a defensive fix only. No behavior changes.

prs.title: Fix maximize handler affecting only last status header column
prs.body:
- Move the maximize handler outside the turtle loop so it applies to every header cell.
- Store the header cells and update their width (and padding/font size on maximize) together.
- Prevents only the last turtle column from resizing when the status widget is maximized.

prs.title: Palette: replace eval-based icon lookup with explicit mapping
prs.body:
## Summary
Replace eval-based palette icon lookup with an explicit mapping to improve maintainability and avoid dynamic evaluation in palette rendering.

## Changes
- Added `buildPaletteImageMap()` to replace dynamic `eval()` usage for palette icon resolution

prs.title: Remove stray token-like string from developer howto
prs.body:
This removes the standalone string (`wi1meeL9Mahdeewu`) from sugar-labs-developer-howto.txt.

The string appears without context and resembles a token or credential. Even if not a secret, such unexplained strings can trigger automated security scanners and cause confusion for readers.

Removing it improves documentation clarity and avoids false positives.

prs.title: Remove stray token-like string from developer howto
prs.body:
## Description

This removes an unexplained token-like line (`wi1meeL9Mahdeewu`) at the end of the developer howto. Even if it wasn’t a real secret, it created security ambiguity and could trigger automated secret scanners. The change keeps the doc clean and avoids false alarms.

prs.title: Harden JS editor console rendering and modernize Lilypond clipboard copy
prs.body:
### Summary This PR improves security and compatibility in the JavaScript editor console and Lilypond save flow. - Render JS editor console output using DOM nodes (textContent with ``/`
`) instead of innerHTML, preventing HTML injection while preserving existing formatting. - Use the modern Clipboard API (navigator.clipboard.writeText) for Lilypond copy, with a fallback to execCommand for compatibility. ### Testing - Verified console output renders HTML-like input as plain text. - Confirmed line spacing remains unchanged. - npm test - npm run lint

prs.title: test: add integration test for taxonomy file structural validation
prs.body:
### What

This PR adds an integration test that validates taxonomy files to prevent syntax regressions introduced through manual edits.

The test performs lightweight structural validation (duplicate properties, malformed lines, unexpected line formats) and is intentionally independent of the taxonomy parsing logic. Its goal is early detection of common errors during CI rather than full parser validation.

This change affects testing only and does not modify runtime behavior.

prs.title: Fix crash in document serve view when storage backend does not implement file size
prs.body:
<!-- For guidance on making a great pull request, be sure to check https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md -->

### Description

This PR fixes a crash in the document serve view (wagtail/documents/views/serve.py) where accessing doc.file.size directly could raise an AttributeError or NotImplementedError when used with storage backends that do not implement file size (for example, certain remote or custom storage backends).

### Changes

The file size is now retrieved safely when setting the Content-Length header:

* The cached file_size attribute on the Document model is used when available.

* Otherwise, doc.file.size is accessed within a try/except block catching (AttributeError, NotImplementedError).

* If the file size cannot be determined, the Content-Length header is not set instead of raising an exception.

This allows documents to be served successfully when the storage backend does not provide file size information.

### Tests

Added test coverage in wagtail/documents/tests/test_views.py to verify:

- Normal serving when size is available.

- Successful serving when accessing size raises AttributeError.

- Correct use of the cached file_size attribute when present.

prs.title: Add install button for PWA using deferredPrompt
prs.body:
Fixes #5405 
This PR completes the existing PWA install flow by exposing a minimal
install UI when the browser fires the `beforeinstallprompt` event.

Previously, the install prompt event was captured but never surfaced
to users. This change adds a small, non-intrusive install button that
appears only when installation is possible and triggers the prompt
via a user gesture, as required by browsers.

Changes:
- Show install button only when `beforeinstallprompt` fires
- Trigger `deferredPrompt.prompt()` on user click
- Await `userChoice` and clear state after use
- Hide install UI once the prompt is handled

This does not alter existing behavior and only makes the already
captured install capability usable.

prs.title: Fix deprecated CSS rules and improve standards compliance
prs.body:
## Overview

This PR fixes #N/A by cleaning up deprecated and ineffective CSS rules and adds missing standard property declarations to improve standards compliance.

## Essential Checklist

Please follow the [instructions for making a code change](https://github.com/oppia/oppia/wiki/Make-a-pull-request).

- [ ] I have linked the issue that this PR fixes in the "Development" section of the sidebar.
- [ ] I have checked the "Files Changed" tab and confirmed that the changes are what I want to make.
- [ ] I have written tests for my code.
- [ ] The **PR title** starts with "Fix #bugnum: " or "Fix part of #bugnum: ...", followed by a short, clear summary of the changes.
- [ ] I have assigned the correct reviewers to this PR (or will leave a comment with the phrase "@{{reviewer_username}} PTAL" if I can't assign them directly).

## Proof that changes are correct
The changes are limited strictly to CSS standards compliance:
- Deprecated CSS at-rules were removed where modern equivalents exist
- Standard CSS properties were added alongside vendor-prefixed ones
- Ineffective or conflicting declarations were removed
- No selectors, layout logic, or functional behavior were changed

## Testing instructions
1. Run the Oppia server locally
2. Navigate through learner and creator pages
3. Verify that there are no visual regressions

## PR Pointers

- Never force push! If you do, your PR will be closed.
- To reply to reviewers, follow these instructions: https://github.com/oppia/oppia/wiki/Rules-for-making-PRs#step-5-address-review-comments-until-all-reviewers-approve
- Some e2e tests are flaky, and can fail for reasons unrelated to your PR. We are working on fixing this, but in the meantime, if you need to restart the tests, please check the ["If your build fails" wiki page](https://github.com/oppia/oppia/wiki/If-CI-checks-fail-on-your-PR).
- See the [Code Owner's wiki page](https://github.com/oppia/oppia/wiki/Oppia's-code-owners-and-checks-to-be-carried-out-by-developers) for what code owners will expect.

prs.title: Fix deprecated CSS rules and improve standards compliance
prs.body:
## Overview

This PR fixes #N/A by cleaning up deprecated and ineffective CSS rules and adds missing standard property declarations to improve standards compliance.

## Essential Checklist

Please follow the [instructions for making a code change](https://github.com/oppia/oppia/wiki/Make-a-pull-request).

prs.title: Fix ReferenceError when doSearch is not yet available
prs.body:
This PR fixes a ReferenceError that occurs when `doSearch()` is invoked
before it is available on page load.

In some load orders, `doSearch` is defined later (e.g. under
`window.activity`), causing the initial call in `$(document).ready()`
to fail and stop further script execution.

The fix safely checks for `doSearch` in both locations before calling
it, preventing the error while preserving existing behavior.

Fixes #5313

issues.title: Fix startup crashes, enforce SECRET_KEY in production, and unify upload limits
issues.body:
## Summary
The application can fail to start due to missing imports and a faulty exception block referencing redis before it exists. The app also falls back to an insecure default SECRET_KEY in non-production and sets conflicting upload limits. These issues risk production downtime and accidental insecure deployments.

## Steps to reproduce
- Without redis / flask-limiter installed:
   ```
   python -m pip install -r requirements.txt
   python app.py
   # observe NameError / ImportError on startup
  ```
- In production without SECRET_KEY:
  ```
   FLASK_ENV=production python app.py
   # should fail with a clear message but currently may crash or behave insecurely
  ```

## Files to change
- app.py
- test_app.py
- docker-compose.yml

issues.title: [BUG] Aggregation ignores evaluator weights / duplicate semantic evaluator
issues.body:
## Description
A mismatch between evaluator identifiers and the aggregator causes evaluator weights to be ignored and final pipeline scores to be incorrect. There was also a duplicate semantic evaluator implementation causing inconsistent behavior across runs.

## Reproduction Steps
1. Provide a resume and a job description (so JD mode is used).
2. Run the pipeline that calls evaluators: from repo root run a Node snippet:
     ```
      node -e "import('./ai-ml/pipeline/runPipeline.js').then(m=>m.runPipeline({ resumeData:{ resumeText: 'Experienced Node dev...' }, jobDescription: 'Looking for a Node developer with AWS and testing experience' })).then(r=>console.log(JSON.stringify(r.breakdown,null,2))).catch(console.error)"
      ```
3. Inspect the printed breakdown and the score.
4. Compare evaluator outputs carefully: several evaluators return identifiers like key (e.g. skill_match) or label, while ai-ml/config/weights.config.js expects camelCase evaluator names such as skillMatch and semanticMatch.

Previously, the aggregator relied only on evalResult.name, which caused weight mappings from weights.config.js to be skipped for evaluators whose identifiers did not match the expected camelCase names.

## Expected Behavior
The aggregator now correctly applies weights from `ai-ml/config/weights.config.js` regardless of whether an evaluator returns `name`, `key`, or `label`.

Semantic evaluation has been consolidated into a single consistent implementation with a stable result contract, including:

* `key: "semanticMatch"`
* numeric `score` (or `null` when unavailable)
* `meta.rateLimited` for Hugging Face rate-limit fallbacks


## Environment Details
- OS: macOS
- Browser: Chrome

issues.title: [FEATURE] Socket.IO rate limiter (opt-in) with configurable warning/cooldown
issues.body:
## Current Problem
WebSocket clients can flood the server with events causing resource exhaustion and abuse; no per-socket throttling or warnings exist today.

## Proposed Solution
Add an opt-in, token-bucket per-socket rate limiter that:

- Emits a rate_warning (soft warning) when a socket nears its quota (configurable %).
- Enforces a rate_limited disconnect when quota is exceeded.
- Provides a cooldown between successive warnings so clients can back off.
- Is enabled/disabled via env and validated at startup.

## Expected Impact
- Prevents message floods and abuse from misbehaving clients.
- Improves platform resilience with minimal UX disruption (clients get a warning before disconnect).
- Low-risk change (opt-in; conservative defaults).

## Implementation Ideas
* Added `server/src/middleware/socketRateLimiter.js` implementing a per-socket token-bucket rate limiter with `rate_warning`, cooldown handling, and `rate_limited` disconnect support.

* Updated `server/src/config/validateEnv.js` to validate all new `SOCKET_RATE_*` environment variables.

* Added `server/test/socketRateLimiter.test.js` integration test to simulate socket flooding and verify limiter behavior.

* Updated `.env.example` with new rate limiter environment variables and default values.

* Updated `server/package.json` dev dependencies to include the Socket.IO test client.

issues.title: [BUG] App can crash during startup when browser storage is unavailable
issues.body:
## Description
The app reads theme preference from localStorage during initial boot. In browsers or environments where storage access is blocked, disabled, or throws a security/quota error, the app can fail before the UI renders. This prevents users from reaching the application at all.

## Reproduction Steps
1. Open the app in a browser profile where local storage is restricted or unavailable.
2. Clear site data or use a privacy mode / hardened browser configuration.
3. Reload the app.
4. Observe that the page may fail to initialize instead of falling back to a default theme.


## Expected Behavior
The app should always start successfully. If theme preference cannot be read, it should fall back to a safe default and continue rendering normally.

## Environment Details
- OS: macOS
- Browser: Chrome

issues.title: [BUG] ai-ml: tests fail when optional deps (zod / mongoose) are not installed
issues.body:
## Description
- `ai-ml` test runs and local CI fail when optional packages (notably `zod` and mongoose) are not installed. Static ESM imports cause `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND` and the test run aborts instead of gracefully skipping or falling back.

## Reproduction Steps
1. Run tests:
   ```bash
   cd ai-ml
   node --test```

2. Observe errors such as:
    ```
    - Error [ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND]: Cannot find package 'mongoose' imported from ai-ml/evaluators/semanticEvaluator.js
    - Error [ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND]: Cannot find package 'zod' imported from ai-ml/pipeline/evaluatorContract.js
    ```
## Expected Behavior
- Tests and local dev runs succeed in a minimal environment (no DB, no HF API token, no optional packages).
- Modules that require optional services:
  - return safe/skippable results when services or API keys are missing, or
  - throw clear, actionable errors only when truly required.
- Test runner should not crash due to missing optional dev/runtime deps.

## Environment Details
- OS: macOS
- Browser: Chrome

issues.title: Redis outage disables rate-limiter & cache, enabling DoS / resource exhaustion
issues.body:
## Problem

The application currently relies on Redis for both rate limiting and caching. When Redis becomes unavailable, these protections are silently disabled instead of falling back to a safe alternative.

As a result, resource-intensive endpoints such as `/api/analyze` can receive an unlimited number of requests, allowing malicious or misbehaving clients to overwhelm the server and exhaust CPU, memory, and I/O resources.

The main symptoms are:

* Rate-limiting decorators remain attached to endpoints but are no longer enforced.
* Redis-backed caching is disabled.
* The application continues serving requests without clearly indicating that important protections are no longer active.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Make Redis unavailable (stop `redis-server` or configure an invalid Redis host).
2. Start the application.
3. Verify Redis is unavailable:

   * Check `GET /health`, or
   * Inspect application logs for Redis connection warnings.
4. Send multiple concurrent requests to a resource-intensive endpoint:

```bash
for i in {1..50}; do
  curl -s -X POST \
    -F "file=@/path/to/sample.jpg" \
    http://localhost:5000/api/analyze &
done
wait
```

5. Observe that the server accepts and processes a large number of requests simultaneously without rate limiting, causing increased CPU and memory usage.

## Expected Behavior

The application should not silently disable protections when Redis is unavailable. Acceptable approaches include:

* **Fail Closed:** Refuse to start (or report an unhealthy state) until Redis is available.
* **Graceful Fallback:** Use an in-memory rate limiter and cache to maintain basic protection in single-process deployments.
* **Operational Visibility:** Emit clear logs, metrics, and alerts indicating that the application is running in a degraded mode.

This ensures operators are aware of the issue and prevents unbounded traffic from overwhelming the service.

**Desktop:**
 - OS: MacOS
 - Browser: Chrome

issues.title: Batch upload blocks requests and is not resilient
issues.body:
## Problem 
The current `/api/batch_upload` flow processes images synchronously inside the HTTP request, causing long blocking requests, timeouts, high memory/CPU usage and unreliable persistence of partial results. There is no per-image retry/backoff, no reliable finalization step, and no scalable background worker orchestration.

## Steps to reproduce
1. Start the app locally (e.g. flask run or however you run the server).
2. POST multiple images to the batch endpoint (example):
    >`curl -X POST -F "files=@img1.jpg" -F "files=@img2.jpg"`
    http://localhost:5000/api/batch_upload
3. Observe that the HTTP request may block for a long time or time out, the server process CPU/memory spikes, and /api/batch_status/ or /api/batch_results/ either do not update or only contain partial/inconsistent results.

## Expected Behaviour

* `/api/batch_upload` should return immediately with a `job_id` and must not block the client request.
* Image processing should run asynchronously through a background worker (preferably Celery), with each image handled as an independent task supporting retries and exponential backoff.
* Each processed image should persist an idempotent `AnalysisResult` record, ensuring partial progress is durable, recoverable, and queryable.
* A reliable finalization mechanism should determine overall job completion and update `BatchJob` statistics (`completed` and `failed` counts).
* Processing progress should be accessible via `/api/batch_status/<job_id>`, while completed results should be retrievable through `/api/batch_results/<job_id>` throughout the job lifecycle.
* Requests exceeding `MAX_BATCH_SIZE` should be rejected with a clear and descriptive error response.
* If Celery or Redis is unavailable, the system should fall back to a safe non-blocking execution model with documented scalability limitations, rather than performing processing synchronously within the request handler.

issues.title: Lock down /admin/models/* to authenticated `researcher`/admin users
issues.body:
**Description**

Currently model-management APIs under /admin/models/* can be invoked without proper authorization, enabling unauthorized users to register/activate/delete models or change A/B/rollback settings.

**Expected Behaviour**

- Unauthenticated requests → JSON 401 or redirect to login (browser).
- Authenticated non-researcher users → 403.
- Authenticated `researcher` or admin users → allowed.

**Files to Review**

app.py, models.py, tests/test_admin_auth.py (new file added)

issues.title: Enforce SECRET_KEY & Harden Session Cookies (fail-fast when missing)
issues.body:
## Description
The app currently falls back to a hard-coded development secret when `SECRET_KEY` is not set (e.g. `dev_secret_123`), and  docker-compose.yml provides the same default. This allows session forgery and accidental insecure deployments.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Unset `SECRET_KEY` in environment.
2. Start the app (production mode).
3. Observe app starts with a predictable/fallback secret or continues running insecurely.

## Files to change
- app.py — enforce and validate `SECRET_KEY`, set cookie hardening defaults.
- docker-compose.yml — remove dev fallback for `SECRET_KEY`.
- .env.example, README.md— document generation and required env var.
- `tests/test_config.py` — add test to assert app fails to start in production without `SECRET_KEY`.

issues.title: server: validate model registration path to models/ directory
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
/admin/models/register accepted arbitrary filesystem paths in the JSON path field (e.g. passwd). This allowed registering files outside the server models/ directory which could later be passed to torch.load, creating an unsafe-deserialization / RCE risk.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Start the app.
2. POST the following to http://localhost:5000/admin/models/register
    >curl -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"model_type":"resnet","version":"v1.1","path":"/etc/passwd"}' \
  http://localhost:5000/admin/models/register
3. Observe the endpoint accepts the request and registers the path.

**Expected behavior**
Requests with path not equal to or under the server models/ directory should be rejected with HTTP 400 and a clear error, e.g. `Invalid model path: must be inside the server 'models/' directory.`

**Desktop:**
 - OS: MacOS
 - Browser: chrome

issues.title: [Docs] Misnamed duplicate docs
issues.body:
**Description**
Repository contains misnamed / duplicate documentation files that confuse tooling and contributors:
- CODE_OF_ CONDUCT.md (space in filename) — duplicate of CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md
- CONTRIUBUTION.md (typo) — duplicate of CONTRIBUTING.md

**Problem**
Duplicate and mispelled docs cause confusion and hurt codebase readability.

**Proposed solution**
Remove the misnamed duplicate files from the repo root:
* Delete CODE_OF_ CONDUCT.md
* Delete CONTRIUBUTION.md

issues.title: [BUG] Critical code quality issues: unhandled promises, unsafe type assertions, and a11y violations
issues.body:
**Bug description**
Multiple critical issues causing unhandled promise rejections, unsafe type assertions, missing error handling, and accessibility violations across 9 files.

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Open DevTools Console
2. Use app (import video, export with overlay, click email input)
3. Observe unhandled rejection warnings

**Expected behavior**
- All promises have `.catch()` handlers
- Type assertions use safe optional chaining (`?.` not `!.!`)
- All errors logged with context
- Email validation with feedback
- WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility compliance
- No memory leaks on file switching

**Actual behavior**
- Console shows unhandled promise rejections
- Type assertions can crash on undefined values
- Errors fail silently with no user feedback
- Email accepts invalid input
- Screen readers can't access all inputs
- Memory accumulates when switching files

**Files affected**
- src/components/LottiePlayer.tsx
- src/components/VideoPreview.tsx
- src/components/Footer.tsx
- src/components/DownloadResult.tsx
- src/components/ImageOverlay.tsx
- src/components/ThumbnailStrip.tsx
- src/components/OnboardingTour.tsx
- src/components/VideoEditor.tsx
- src/lib/ffmpeg.ts

**Browser and OS info**
- OS: macOS 14
- Browser: Chrome

issues.title: Enhance Number Guessing Game UI and UX
issues.body:
### Summary

Improve the CLI UX for the Number Guessing Game to make prompts, feedback and difficulty options clearer. This issue requests a focused polish (wording, icons, validation, hints) and a difficulty section without changing core gameplay.

### Why

- A difficulty feature with attempt limits to make game interesting.
- Clear feedback reduces confusion and improves perceived polish for small CLI projects.

### File Change

`python-mini-project/games/Number-Guessing-Game/Number-Guessing-Game.py`

issues.title: 2048 high score file should use a script-relative path and ignore invalid data
issues.body:
## Description
When the 2048 game starts, it loads and saves highscore.txt using a plain relative path. That makes the score file dependent on the current working directory instead of the game script location.

This can cause two problems:

* the high score may be read from or written to the wrong folder
* the game can crash on startup if the file is empty or contains non-numeric text

## Expected Behavior
The game should always use the correct score file next to the script and should fall back to a default high score of 0 if the file contents are invalid.

## Actual Behavior
The score file can be resolved incorrectly, and loading can fail with a value conversion error.

issues.title: [BUG] Inconsistent default editor behavior due to duplicated DEFAULT_RECIPE definitions
issues.body:
**Bug description**
The app defines DEFAULT_RECIPE in more than one place with different values. This creates ambiguity about which defaults are actually used and can cause silent behavior drift in the editor.

**Steps to reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Open the codebase and locate DEFAULT_RECIPE definitions.
2. Compare the values in src/lib/types.ts and src/lib/constants.ts.
3. Notice that the defaults differ.
4. Start the app and observe that the editor behavior depends on which definition is imported.

**Expected behavior**
There should be a single authoritative DEFAULT_RECIPE definition, and all parts of the app should use the same defaults consistently.

**Actual behavior**
DEFAULT_RECIPE is duplicated with different values, which can lead to inconsistent editor defaults and confusing behavior.

**Browser and OS info**
 - OS: macOS 14
 - Browser: Chrome

issues.title: [BUG] Duplicate Escape Key Event Listener Causes Double Handler Execution and Memory Leak in ExportOverlay
issues.body:
**Bug description**
ExportOverlay.tsx registers the Escape key event handler twice via duplicate useEffect hooks. This causes the cancel callback to execute twice when Escape is pressed and creates a memory leak when the component remounts, as event listeners accumulate without being properly deduplicated.

**Steps to reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Open the application and trigger a video export
2. Wait for the export overlay modal to appear
3. Press the Escape key
4. Observe that the export is cancelled (check network tab or add console.log to onCancel)
5. Reopen the export dialog and press Escape again
6. Note that the cancel callback still fires twice on each press

**Expected behavior**
Pressing Escape should trigger the cancel callback exactly once and cleanly close the export overlay without duplicate executions.

**Actual behavior**
Pressing Escape triggers the cancel callback twice due to two identical event listeners being registered. Over multiple component mounts/unmounts, event listeners accumulate, causing memory leaks and degraded performance.

**Browser and OS info**
 - OS: macOS 14
 - Browser: Chrome

issues.title: DOM-XSS in search history and FLAMES result rendering
issues.body:
**Description** 

The web app renders user-controlled input with innerHTML in two places, which allows injected markup to execute in the browser.

In web-app/js/main.js, recent search terms are saved to localStorage and later inserted into the recent-search dropdown as HTML. A crafted search string can persist and execute when the dropdown is rendered.

In web-app/js/projects/flames.js, the entered names are interpolated into the FLAMES result card using innerHTML. A malicious name can break out of the markup and inject scriptable content into the modal.

**Expected behavior**

User input should be rendered as text, not HTML. The recent-search UI and FLAMES results should use safe DOM construction or text escaping.

issues.title: Division by Zero in Typing Speed Tester
issues.body:
### Problem:
The WPM (Words Per Minute) calculation had no guard against time_taken being zero or near-zero:
` wpm = (words / time_taken) * 60 `

### When it occurs:

- User presses Enter immediately after the "Start Typing" prompt
- time_taken becomes 0.00 or extremely close to 0
- Results in either a ZeroDivisionError or inf (infinity) WPM value

issues.title: Fibonacci generator returns incorrect number of terms for user input
issues.body:
## Description

The Fibonacci script in math/Fibonacci-Series/Fibonacci-Series.py does not always respect the number of terms entered by the user.

When a user inputs a value like 5, the script outputs more than 5 terms. This happens because the sequence is pre-initialized with multiple values and then the loop appends additional numbers without properly limiting the final length to exactly n.

## Expected behavior:

If user enters n, the script should output exactly n Fibonacci terms.

issues.title: Add classic Turtle-based Snake game in empty Snake-Game file
issues.body:
## Problem
- The file games/Snake-Game/Snake-Game.py was previously empty, so the project lacks a playable Snake game.

## Proposed Solution
- Add a Turtle-based Snake implementation at games/Snake-Game/Snake-Game.py with arrow-key controls, food spawning, score display, growing segments, and collision/reset logic.

## File Change
- games/Snake-Game/Snake-Game.py

issues.title: Fix CLI long options and logger TypeError
issues.body:
- In scripts/upload.py, the long options are defined as `-path` and `-url`, which argparse does not treat as valid long options. They should be`--path` and `--url`.

- In exporter.py, the logger call concatenates a string with an exception object:
  logger.error("export failed: " + ex)

  This can raise a TypeError if `ex` is not a string.

If this seems reasonable, I would start a PR to address these.

issues.title: Fix file descriptor leak and ensure consistent return values in LocalExporter
issues.body:
## Summary
The `LocalExporter` implementation has two reliability issues:

1. File handles may remain open if an exception occurs during write.
2. The method returns None instead of boolean values, while other exporters return True on success and False on failure.

## Problems

- In long-running processes, unclosed file handles may accumulate if exceptions occur during writes.
- Callers that rely on truthy return values cannot reliably detect successful exports.
- The behaviour differs from other exporters, making the interface inconsistent and harder to reason about.

## Affected File

`murakami/exporters/local.py`

---

If this looks good, I can open a PR with the fixes.

issues.title: Fix broken wiki placeholders and add authoritative references
issues.body:
### Problem
Several documentation pages still contain unresolved wiki-era placeholders such as
`Category:FIXME` and internal links that no longer resolve.

These appear to be remnants from the migration away from the OWASP wiki and are
now visible in the rendered markdown.

### Examples 
- `pages/attacks/Spyware.md`
- `pages/attacks/Trojan_Horse.md`
- `pages/attacks/Unicode_Encoding.md`
- `pages/vulnerabilities/Portability_Flaw.md`
- `pages/vulnerabilities/Privacy_Violation.md`
- Other similar markdown files

### Expected outcome
- Remove unresolved `Category:FIXME` placeholders
- Replace broken internal wiki links with valid references where available
- Otherwise remove links that no longer have a meaningful target

### Scope
This is a documentation cleanup only:
- No changes to technical meaning or security guidance
- No structural refactors

I’m happy to take this on in a small, conservative PR if that aligns with the project’s direction.

issues.title: [UI/UX] PWA install prompt captured but never surfaced to users
issues.body:
### Description

Music Blocks currently listens for the beforeinstallprompt event and stores it, but there is no UI or handler to trigger the install flow.

As a result:

- Users cannot install Music Blocks as a PWA even when the browser allows it

- The captured deferredPrompt event is never consumed

- The code partially implements the PWA install pattern but leaves it incomplete

### Steps to observe

1. Open Music Blocks in a browser that supports PWA installation

2. beforeinstallprompt fires and is prevented

3. No install option appears in the UI

4. deferredPrompt.prompt() is never called

### Expected Behavior

When the browser indicates the app is installable, a UI affordance should appear allowing the user to trigger the install prompt via a user gesture.

### Environment

-   Operating System: [e.g., Windows, macOS, Linux]
-   Browser (if applicable): [e.g., Chrome, Firefox, Safari]
-   Version of Software/Project: [e.g., v1.0.0]

### Checklist

-   [ ] I have read and followed the project's code of conduct.
-   [ ] I have searched for similar issues before creating this one.
-   [ ] I have provided enough information to understand the issue.
-   [ ] I am willing to contribute to the resolution of this issue.

---

Thank you for contributing to our project! We appreciate your help in improving it.

📚 See [contributing instructions](https://github.com/sugarlabs/musicblocks/blob/master/README.md).

🙋🏾🙋🏼 Questions: [Community Matrix Server](https://matrix.to/#/#sugar:matrix.org).

issues.title: Donation pages use incorrect utm_content language codes for analytics tracking
issues.body:
### What
  While reviewing donation HTML pages, I noticed that many non-English files
(e.g. pt_PT.html, yi.html, ha.html) use utm_content=donate-page-share-en
even though the page content is not English.

This likely causes incorrect analytics attribution and makes it difficult
to measure which language versions drive donations.

Example:
- pt_PT.html uses donate-page-share-en
- yi.html uses donate-page-share-en

----


### Steps to reproduce the behavior
1. Open a non-English donation HTML file (e.g. pt_PT.html).
2. Inspect the og:url meta tag.
3. Observe that utm_content is set to donate-page-share-en.

-----


### Expected behavior
- utm_content should reflect the language (or language variant) of the donation page
so analytics can correctly attribute donations by language.

----

### Why
- Incorrect utm_content values cause analytics and campaign attribution to be wrong,
making it difficult to understand which language versions drive donations.

----

### Additional context
- This issue was observed across multiple non-English donation pages.
Happy to prepare a small, scoped PR once the expected utm_content format
is confirmed.

issues.title: [Bug] ReferenceError during startup when doSearch is not defined
issues.body:
### Description

A JavaScript ReferenceError can occur during page load in `index.html`
when `doSearch()` is called before it is defined.

This depends on script load order and can prevent subsequent initialization
code from running.

### Expected Behavior

The application should load without JavaScript errors, and `doSearch()` should
only be called when the function is available.

### How to Reproduce

1. Open Music Blocks in a browser.
2. Open DevTools → Console.
3. Reload the page.
4. Observe the ReferenceError depending on script load order.

### Console log Errors:

- `Uncaught ReferenceError: doSearch is not defined`

### Environment:

- Browser: Chrome / Firefox
- OS: Any
- Version: Current `main` branch
